title: Command and Conquer: Red Alert 3 - Uprising (24800)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Command and Conquer: Red Alert 3 - Uprising 
- Steam AppID of the game: 24800

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX 6950 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: `video-linux            2018.05.04`
- Kernel version: 5.15.85-1-MANJARO
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/Industrial/252a678905b709d4fb85f8cdb0109454
- Proton version: 7.0-5 (took stable to be sure)

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

  
https://gist.github.com/Industrial/89c0fc64c5b8693a45f9d298f87079e4

## Symptoms 
"No Language Packs Found"


## Reproduction
When I start the game I get the error message "No Language Packs Found", "Please install at least one language pack"

When I right click on the game and go to Properties > Language and select another language (then English) it will download more data.

When that download is finished I press play again. I get the same error message.

title: Hyper Dash (1386890)
text:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Hyper Dash
- Steam AppID of the game: 1386890
- note: VR game, with optional non-VR spectator mode

## System Information
- GPU: RX 7900 XTX
- Video driver version: Mesa 24.2.7
- Kernel version: 6.11.10-273-tkg-eevdf
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/Patola/4ae9c8cc03500699b3bdf84ad0ec05d5
- Proton version: 9.0-3

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms 
When trying to run in VR mode, it shows an error dialog and bails out.

![failed](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0b1c661f-3ef6-4ef0-9f12-e5a836eb4de2)

Running in non-VR works but this is only a spectator mode, not the game.

## Reproduction

- Run the game in VR. It shows that dialog and exits.
- If the game is ran in non-VR mode, it goes to the menu, but it's spectator-only mode.

Attached are the proton logs for both modes, VR and non-VR spectator mode.
[steam-1386890.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17965663/steam-1386890.log)
[steam-1386890-vr.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/17965664/steam-1386890-vr.log)

title: DOOM: The Dark Ages (3017860)
text: --- # Compatibility Report - Name of the game with compatibility issues: Doom The Dark Ages - Steam AppID of the game: 3017860 ## System Information - CPU: Intel Core i5-13600K with e-cores disabled in bios - GPU: RX 7900 XT - Video driver version: 4.6 (Compatibility Profile) Mesa 25.2.1+git2508221109.71dcc1dc23b~p~mesarc1 - Kernel version: 6.14.4-061404-generic - Kubuntu 25.10 - Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/stellaberrant/d976f08b8fca9a270a03b5439dc855df - Proton version: various, tried Experimental, 9 and Proton GE-10-10 ## I confirm: - [ x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game. - [x ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available. ## Symptoms Since a game update (Either update 2 or update 2.1), I am unable to play the game. It launches but then proceeds to freeze at the "Press Spacebar or (A) to Start" screen. It simply becomes unresponsive entirely and after a few seconds the music stops. Dmesg log: [dmesg_log_splitlock_kubuntu.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22057753/dmesg_log_splitlock_kubuntu.tar.gz) Proton log: [steam-3017860_splitlock_kubuntu.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22057872/steam-3017860_splitlock_kubuntu.tar.gz) Proton log from a working installation: [steam-3017860_ally_working.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22057754/steam-3017860_ally_working.tar.gz) System information as a file: [system information.txt](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22057755/system.information.txt) So far I tried: - deleting the protonprefix - deleting the userdata for the game - disabling the Steam Overlay - unplugging my Dualsense controller - launching with `SteamDeck=1 %command%` - importing a profile.bin from a working installation of Doom TDA - launching with gamescope - launching with `+com_skipkeypressonloadscreens 1 +com_skipIntroVideo 1` - disabling split lock detection before boot - Proton 9, Experimental, and GE 10-10 (and eventually got locked out by Denuvo) - uninstalling and reinstalling the game - skipping shader compilation - updated my whole Kubuntu system which pulled in newer Mesa for example, same issue - I tried to play the game on the same system with Bazzite instead of Kubuntu(Fedora-based, with newer Mesa and Kernel) However, the game is still working on my ROG Ally X which is also running Bazzite. So I'm thinking it's somehow related to hardware maybe? I also attached the Proton log from this system where the game is still working so you can compare the 2 ## Reproduction - launch game - press (A) or Spacebar when prompted - the game freezes and music stops, game needs to be exited via the steam ui **EDIT: I've combed this forum and protondb and reddit for reports, and have found others on Linux with the same issue, as well as one user on Discord. Windows does not seem to be affected. However, they all share an Intel 12/13/14th gen processor in common. Could this be the culprit???**
